formName: NDIS Access Request — Supporting Evidence Form
dictationTips: Describe the primary impairment and its impact on daily life
Mention whether the condition is lifelong or likely to be permanent
Describe functional impact across domains: mobility, communication, social, learning, self-care, self-management
If relevant, mention any standardized assessments and their scores
Note any early intervention supports that could reduce future needs

patientGuardian.label: Parent/Guardian/Carer/Representative
patientGuardian.pdfField: Name of parent guardian carer representative

primaryImpairment.label: 2.1 Primary Impairment
primaryImpairment.pdfField: 21 What is the persons primary impairment ie the impairment with the most impact on daily life
primaryImpairment.llmInstruction: Describe the person's primary impairment — the impairment with the most impact on daily life. 2-3 sentences.

primaryImpairmentDuration.label: 2.2 Duration of Primary Impairment
primaryImpairmentDuration.pdfField: 22 How long has the person had this impairment
primaryImpairmentDuration.llmInstruction: How long the person has had the primary impairment. E.g. 'Since birth', '5 years', '10+ years'.

primaryImpairmentLifelong.label: 2.3 Impairment Likely Lifelong?
primaryImpairmentLifelong.pdfField: 23 Is the impairment likely to be lifelong Note an impairment may be considered likely to be lifelong even if the impact on the functional capacity fluctuates or varies in intensity over time
primaryImpairmentLifelong.llmInstruction: Is the primary impairment likely to be lifelong? An impairment may be lifelong even if functional capacity fluctuates. Respond with 'Yes' or 'No' and a brief explanation.

primaryTreatment.label: 2.4 Treatment Undertaken
primaryTreatment.pdfField: 24 Please provide a brief description of any relevant treatment undertaken current andor past
primaryTreatment.llmInstruction: Brief description of current and/or past treatment for the primary impairment. 2-3 sentences.

secondaryImpairment.label: 2.5 Secondary Impairment
secondaryImpairment.pdfField: 25 Does the person have another impairment that has a significant impact If yes please list
secondaryImpairment.llmInstruction: If the person has another impairment with significant impact, describe it. Leave empty if no secondary impairment mentioned.

secondaryImpairmentDuration.label: 2.6 Duration of Secondary Impairment
secondaryImpairmentDuration.pdfField: 26 How long has the person had this impairment
secondaryImpairmentDuration.llmInstruction: Duration of the secondary impairment. Only fill if a secondary impairment was described.

secondaryImpairmentLifelong.label: 2.7 Secondary Impairment Lifelong?
secondaryImpairmentLifelong.pdfField: 27 Is the impairment likely to be lifelong
secondaryImpairmentLifelong.llmInstruction: Is the secondary impairment likely to be lifelong? Only fill if a secondary impairment was described.

secondaryTreatment.label: 2.8 Secondary Impairment Treatment
secondaryTreatment.pdfField: 28 Please provide a brief of any relevant treatment undertaken current andor past
secondaryTreatment.llmInstruction: Treatment for the secondary impairment. Only fill if a secondary impairment was described.

otherImpairments.label: 2.9 Other Impairments
otherImpairments.pdfField: 29 Does the person have any other impairments If yes please list
otherImpairments.llmInstruction: List any other impairments beyond primary and secondary. Leave empty if none mentioned.

earlyInterventionAlleviate.label: Early Intervention: Alleviate Impact
earlyInterventionAlleviate.pdfField: Alleviate the impact on functional capacity
earlyInterventionAlleviate.llmInstruction: Set to 'yes' if early intervention could alleviate the impact on functional capacity. Otherwise leave empty.

earlyInterventionPrevent.label: Early Intervention: Prevent Deterioration
earlyInterventionPrevent.llmInstruction: Set to 'yes' if early intervention could prevent deterioration of functional capacity. Otherwise leave empty.

earlyInterventionImprove.label: Early Intervention: Improve Capacity
earlyInterventionImprove.llmInstruction: Set to 'yes' if early intervention could improve functional capacity. Otherwise leave empty.

earlyInterventionStrengthen.label: Early Intervention: Strengthen Supports
earlyInterventionStrengthen.pdfField: Strengthen the sustainability of available or
earlyInterventionStrengthen.llmInstruction: Set to 'yes' if early intervention could strengthen existing supports. Otherwise leave empty.

earlyInterventionDetails.label: Early Intervention Details
earlyInterventionDetails.pdfField: Details of recommended early intervention supports
earlyInterventionDetails.llmInstruction: Details of recommended early intervention supports. Only fill if early intervention benefits were indicated.

assessmentCansAttached.llmInstruction: Leave empty — attachment status is handled outside dictation.

questions.label: Is the impairment likely to be lifelong?

yes.primaryImpairmentLifelong: Yes — the impairment is likely to be lifelong.

no.primaryImpairmentLifelong: No — the impairment is not expected to be lifelong.

questions.label: Does the person have a secondary impairment?
Is the secondary impairment likely to be lifelong?

yes.secondaryImpairmentLifelong: Yes — the impairment is likely to be lifelong.

no.secondaryImpairmentLifelong: No — the impairment is not expected to be lifelong.

questions.label: Are early intervention supports likely to benefit the person?

dictationGuide.title: Functional Domains
dictationGuide.description: Indicate which domains are affected by the impairment(s).

systemPromptAdditions:
This is an NDIS Access Request — Supporting Evidence Form. Key instructions:

1. IMPAIRMENT DESCRIPTIONS: Keep text field responses to 2-3 concise sentences. Focus on clinical facts and functional impact.

2. LIFELONG ASSESSMENT: An impairment may be considered lifelong even if its impact fluctuates or varies in intensity over time.

3. EARLY INTERVENTION: Only check early intervention checkboxes if the doctor explicitly discusses benefits of early intervention. Map to the 4 categories: alleviate impact, prevent deterioration, improve capacity, strengthen sustainability.

4. ASSESSMENTS: Only fill assessment date and score fields if the doctor explicitly mentions a standardized assessment by name (e.g. 'Modified Rankin Scale score of 3'). Do NOT guess or infer assessment scores.

5. FUNCTIONAL IMPACT (6 domains): For each domain (mobility, communication, social interaction, learning, self-care, self-management), determine the type of assistance needed:
   - 'No assistance' means the impairment does NOT affect this domain
   - 'Special equipment' = physical equipment (wheelchair, walker, etc.)
   - 'Assistive technology' = electronic/digital aids
   - 'Home modifications' = structural changes to living environment
   - 'Person assistance' = help from another person (physical, guidance, supervision, prompting)
   Multiple assistance types can apply to one domain.

6. SELF-CARE SUB-AREAS: Only check specific self-care areas (showering, eating, overnight care, toileting, dressing) when the doctor explicitly mentions difficulty with that activity.

7. For checkbox fields, output 'yes' to check or leave the field empty/omit it to leave unchecked.
allowedUnmappedPdfFields: Does the person require assistance with selfcare because of their impairments No does not need assistance
